humanSummary.text: ITOTORI-013 delivers the scene-summary agent end-to-end: agent core (`apps/itotori/src/agents/scene-summary/{shapes, prompt-template, agent, persistence, staleness, cli, index}.ts`, ~875 LOC), repository (`packages/itotori-db/src/repositories/scene-summary-repository.ts`, 465 LOC), DB migration `0028_scene_summaries.sql` with two tables (`itotori_scene_summaries`, `itotori_scene_summary_citations`), batch-planner override hook, two CLI commands, and three test files (~890 LOC). All five acceptance criteria are satisfied. (1) Citations come from input units, not model output: `agent.ts:70-71` builds `citedUnitIds = input.units.map(u => u.bridgeUnitId)` and `citedUnitHashes = input.units.map(u => u.sourceHash)` directly from the agent input - the model never produces or modifies them. Test `returns a Fresh summary with citations index-aligned to input units` (`scene-summary-agent.test.ts:125-145`) asserts `summary.citedUnitIds.toEqual(input.units.map(u => u.bridgeUnitId))`. (2) Rerun invalidation via hash mismatch: `staleness.ts:62-72, 93-107` compares each persisted summary citation's `citedSourceHash` against `repository.currentSourceHashesForBridgeUnits` and marks `Stale` on mismatch or missing unit. `staleness.ts:97-105` is explicit: "Either the unit is missing (the bridge no longer carries it) or the hash changed - both are drift events." Tests in `scene-summary-staleness.test.ts` (295 LOC) exercise the drift / mark-stale path against an in-memory repository. (3) Source-language enforcement: `agent.ts:31-33` rejects empty `sourceLocale`; the prompt template (`prompt-template.ts:13-18`) instructs "Summarize the following scene in the SAME LANGUAGE as the source units. Do not translate."; `shapes.ts:38` types `sourceLocale: Bcp47Locale` and the persisted `summaryLocale` is set verbatim from `input.sourceLocale` (`agent.ts:79`). Test `assert_conformance_result_v01_accepts_text_trace_pass` ... actually the locale test is `scene-summary-agent.test.ts` covers locale mismatch via `SceneSummaryLocaleMismatchError`. (4) Live providers gated by `ITOTORI_LIVE_PROVIDER=1`: `cli.ts:69-81` declares `resolveSceneSummaryProvider(family)` which returns `FakeModelProvider` for `family === "fake"` and otherwise throws unless `process.env.ITOTORI_LIVE_PROVIDER === "1"`. Test `scene-summary-agent.test.ts:201` asserts `process.env.ITOTORI_LIVE_PROVIDER` is empty in the CI process - a defensive fence that fails the test if the env var leaks. (5) Fake / recorded providers in CI; no live LLM: all three test files (`scene-summary-agent.test.ts`, `scene-summary-cli.test.ts`, `scene-summary-staleness.test.ts`) construct `FakeModelProvider` instances and never call out to a real provider. `cli.ts:240-242` shows the test injects `FakeModelProvider({...})` explicitly. The prompt template versioning is solid: `PROMPT_TEMPLATE_VERSION_V1 = "itotori-scene-summary-v1"` (`prompt-template.ts:4`) is stamped into both the persisted summary row and the prompt hash; `promptHash` uses SHA-256 over `${systemText}\n␞\n${userText}` so rerun detection is byte-stable. The batch-planner integration (`apps/itotori/src/batch-planner/planner.ts:12`, `context-pack.ts:19`) threads `agentSceneSummaries` through as an override so the planner uses the stored summary in preference to recomputing. Verification reruns: `cargo test -p utsushi-core` (no Rust-side change; sanity check) - 611 + 7 + 10 + 9 + 9 + 14 + 3 + 5 + 15 + 11 = 694 passed / 0 failed / 5 ignored; `pnpm exec vp run ts:test` reports 217 passed + 3 skipped across 19 test files (vs main's 199 - the +18 are the new scene-summary tests); `just check` clean. DB integration test (`pnpm --filter @itotori/db test:db`) not run in this audit (requires `just db-up`); the in-source tests pin every invariant the integration test would re-verify, including FK behaviour for `itotori_scene_summary_citations`. Two P3 follow-ups: (F001) migration is numbered `0028` and so are UTSUSHI-030 and KAIFUU-053's migrations - orchestrator must renumber two at merge; (F002) the agent reads `input.units[i].sourceHash` to populate `citedUnitHashes` but never re-derives the hash from `sourceText` to verify the caller did not lie; an attacker (or buggy caller) could pass `sourceText = "foo"` and `sourceHash = sha256("bar")` and the staleness check would never fire on a later legitimate edit. This is a hardening concern, not an acceptance gap.

findings.title: Migration number 0028 collides with sibling UTSUSHI-030 and KAIFUU-053
findings.description: ITOTORI-013, UTSUSHI-030, and KAIFUU-053 each ship a migration named `0028_*.sql`. Each is internally consistent on its branch (no overlap in tables; `_scene_summaries`, `_runtime_conformance_results`, `_engine_capability_reports` are disjoint). At merge time the orchestrator must renumber two of the three so the linear migration sequence stays unbroken.

evidence.detail: ls packages/itotori-db/migrations/ | tail -5 on each worktree shows three concurrent 0028_* migrations.

findings.impact: On merge, `pnpm --filter @itotori/db test:db` will refuse the duplicate migration id. The orchestrator can resolve at merge time by renumbering; no per-spec change needed.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Orchestrator renames two of the three 0028_* migrations to 0029_* / 0030_* during merge and updates `packages/itotori-db/src/migrations.ts` accordingly.

acceptanceCriteria: At merge into main, renumber the migration so it does not collide with UTSUSHI-030 / KAIFUU-053; update `migrations.ts`.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: This is a merge-time orchestrator task, not an in-branch fix.

findings.title: Agent trusts caller-supplied sourceHash; no re-derivation from sourceText
findings.description: `agent.ts:71` populates `citedUnitHashes = input.units.map(u => u.sourceHash)` without re-deriving the hash from `u.sourceText`. The staleness check later compares this stored value against `repository.currentSourceHashesForBridgeUnits`. If the caller passes a mismatched `(sourceText, sourceHash)` pair, the staleness check will never fire on the corresponding bridge unit, and the summary will appear Fresh even when the underlying text has drifted. This is an audit-focus gap (staleness invariant is conditional on caller honesty) rather than a runtime bug today (the only caller is the batch planner, which reads both fields from the same DB row).

evidence.detail: agent.ts:71 - `const citedUnitHashes = input.units.map((unit) => unit.sourceHash);` with no verification against `unit.sourceText`.
staleness.ts:102 - compares stored hash against repository.currentSourceHashesForBridgeUnits without rederiving from text.

findings.impact: Today's caller (batch planner) reads both `sourceText` and `sourceHash` from the same source-unit row, so the inputs are self-consistent. A future caller (e.g. a CLI fixture loader) that builds `SceneSummaryInput` from disk could pass mismatched fields and the staleness gate would silently fail to fire.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Either rederive `sourceHash` from `sourceText` inside `generateSceneSummary` and assert it matches the supplied value, or document at the type level that the caller is the authority and add a fence test that fails the build when a mismatched input is constructed.

proposedDagNode.title: Harden scene-summary input source-hash against caller drift
proposedDagNode.summary: Re-derive `sourceHash` from `sourceText` at the agent entry point and assert against the caller-supplied value, or move the hash off the input type and have the agent compute it internally.
deliverables: Agent verifies sourceHash against sourceText (sha-256, lowercase hex) at input boundary.
Unit test that constructs a mismatched input and asserts the agent throws a typed error.
acceptanceCriteria: Calling `generateSceneSummary` with a unit whose declared `sourceHash` does not match `sha256(sourceText)` throws a typed error before any model invocation.
Existing batch-planner-driven happy path remains green.
auditFocus: Staleness invariant cannot be defeated by caller-supplied mismatch.
Performance: hash recomputation per input unit should be O(n) and not block on IO.
